title: Effects of yoga versus walking on mood, anxiety, and brain GABA levels: a randomized controlled MRS study
journal: Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y.)
headline: Effects of yoga versus walking on mood, anxiety, and brain GABA levels: a randomized controlled MRS study
summary: A randomized controlled trial of yoga examined brain & mechanisms. This is the first study to demonstrate that increased thalamic GABA levels are associated with improved mood and decreased anxiety. It is also the first time that a behavioral intervention (i.e., yoga postures) has been associated with a positive correlation between acute increases in thalamic GABA levels and improvements in mood and anxiety scales.
keyFindings: The 12-week yoga intervention was associated with greater improvements in mood and anxiety than a metabolically matched walking exercise.
This is the first study to demonstrate that increased thalamic GABA levels are associated with improved mood and decreased anxiety.

It is also the first time that a behavioral intervention (i.e., yoga postures) has been associated with a positive correlation between acute increases in thalamic GABA levels and improvements in mood and anxiety scales.
limitations: Single-trial result; requires replication, and self-reported outcomes may inflate apparent effects.
The practice studied (yoga) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
